goal: Define the corrective Hook safety and privacy contract.
description: Scope: establish the user-visible contract. Context: repeated cross-project Cursor deadlock and the completed privacy audit. Target: explicit activation, fail-open ambiguity, bounded continuation, private relative evidence, opt-in installation, and one current interface. Design Considerations: keep hard guarantees in the manifest state machine. Design Value: prevent a global adapter from disabling unrelated work. Constraints & Risks: preserve unrelated host configuration.

acceptance_criteria.text: Requirements define scope, non-goals, and final acceptance.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Requirements.md records the corrective delivery contract.

goal: Ground the correction in observed behavior, source, and current host protocols.
description: Scope: collect only redacted evidence. Context: user reports, audited source paths, and official lifecycle contracts. Target: explain the failure mechanism and the safe response. Design Considerations: distinguish verified behavior from inference. Design Value: prevent recurrence through evidence-backed boundaries. Constraints & Risks: do not persist local paths or runtime output.

acceptance_criteria.text: Evidence cites the observed failure, relevant source, and current host contracts.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Evidence.md contains redacted repository and official-document evidence.

goal: Map every Hook safety requirement to focused and final proof.
description: Scope: define verification before implementation. Context: existing tests passed while missing deadlock and privacy cases. Target: focused behavior tests, a one-time removal search, and one final suite. Design Considerations: validate intended invariants rather than retain historical counterexamples. Design Value: turn the corrective contract into executable proof. Constraints & Risks: full regression runs only in final validation.

acceptance_criteria.text: Every requirement maps to focused and final proof.

goal: Define the activation, continuation, evidence, and delivery boundaries.
description: Scope: establish the corrective architecture. Context: workflow, protocol, configuration, and delivery modules already exist. Target: preserve dependency direction while changing global fail-closed behavior to scoped fail-open behavior. Design Considerations: functional core with thin adapters, unique workspace selection, digested evidence, and explicit installation intent. Design Value: minimize coupling while reducing blast radius. Constraints & Risks: no duplicate runtime or compatibility wrapper.

acceptance_criteria.text: Architecture defines module ownership and all safety contracts.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Architecture.md records the current target boundaries.

goal: Make Hook activation automatic, repository-scoped, fail-open, and bounded.
description: Scope: Closure: capability - dedicated project detection and bounded lifecycle enforcement. Context: broad recursive discovery and mandatory ambiguity continuation deadlocked unrelated Cursor work. Target: run one automatic detector before Hook logic, accept only one structural Better Plan workspace in the current repository, enforce only one active Node, allow state recovery, honor host continuation guards, and return empty output on ambiguity or inspection failure. Design Considerations: keep project detection in hook_scope, host translation in hook_tool, and state loading in manifest_tool. Design Value: retain automatic lifecycle feedback without global denial or manual project switches. Constraints & Risks: never choose a concurrent Node or expose exception details.

acceptance_criteria.text: The dedicated detector automatically activates only for one structural Better Plan workspace in the current repository.
No workspace, multiple workspaces, multiple active Nodes, malformed state, and ambiguous roots return an empty response while state recovery remains executable.
Codex and Claude stop_hook_active and Cursor loop_count bound continuation to one callback.
Focused Hook and discovery tests pass.

goal: Make paths and evidence private by construction.
description: Scope: Closure: capability - privacy-safe path and evidence serialization. Context: CLI and evidence channels retained concrete paths, commands, and failure output. Target: require repository-relative file evidence, store command digests, discard output, validate bounded safe summaries, and render CLI paths relative to a relevant root. Design Considerations: central helpers and schema validation prevent scattered redaction. Design Value: eliminate disclosure rather than depend on operator discipline. Constraints & Risks: migrate current state records once and retain no migration utility.

acceptance_criteria.text: Command evidence stores only a digest, success, and timestamp; all command output is discarded.
File evidence is repository-relative and confined to the project root; free text rejects concrete path or secret-like data.
Manifest CLI output and errors do not expose concrete machine paths.
Focused manifest tests pass after the one-time state-record migration.

goal: Make automatic Hook delivery portable, detector-gated, and single-interface.
description: Scope: Closure: capability - safe local-agent delivery. Context: install/update enabled global Hooks that lacked an automatic project gate, printed concrete paths, retained a duplicate update wrapper and aliases, and permanently checked a removed fallback. Target: automatic managed handlers that always enter through the dedicated detector, portable commands, logical diagnostics, scoped removal, and one installer entry point. Design Considerations: preserve unrelated JSON handlers and the existing single skill tree. Design Value: retain zero-configuration project detection while eliminating global enforcement and compatibility debt. Constraints & Risks: keep the user's local Hooks uninstalled after syncing the skill.

acceptance_criteria.text: Install and update deliver detector-gated managed handlers automatically; scoped removal preserves unrelated handlers.
Generated configuration, adapter text, installer output, and doctor output contain no concrete machine paths.
The duplicate update entry point, installer aliases, and permanent fallback-removal gate are absent, proven by a one-time search.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: One-time repository removal audit found no obsolete wrapper, alias, fallback gate, or discovery interface.

acceptance_criteria.text: Focused installer tests and skill validation pass with updated documentation.

commit.message: Make Better Plan Hook delivery detector-gated

goal: Prove the corrected Hook and privacy boundaries end to end.
description: Scope: final validation of all Hook safety requirements. Context: each implementation closure has its own focused proof. Target: run the complete suite once, validate plan structure and labels, perform the one-time static removal/privacy search, and confirm local managed Hook count remains zero. Design Considerations: retain only redacted counts and digests. Design Value: close the corrective plan against its original requirements. Constraints & Risks: do not reinstall Hooks during local skill synchronization.

acceptance_criteria.text: The complete unit suite passes once after all focused checks.
Workspace validation and requirement-label checks pass.
One-time static checks find no removed interface, concrete local path, secret, or runtime-data artifact.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: One-time static audit found no obsolete interface, concrete local path, secret-shaped value, network runtime address, or generated runtime artifact.

acceptance_criteria.text: The updated local skill matches the repository while all managed Better Plan Hook counts remain zero.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Installed skill hashes match the repository and managed Hook counts are zero for Codex, Cursor, and Claude.
